data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the project structure. Let me create all the necessary files:

1. A `Models/Recipe.cs` with the data model
2. A `Services/RecipeService.cs` with in-memory recipe data
3. Update `Home.razor` - homepage with categories
4. `Pages/Category.razor` - browse recipes by category (static SSR)
5. `Pages/RecipeDetail.razor` - individual recipe page with star rating
6. `Pages/SubmitRecipe.razor` - form to submit a recipe (interactive)
7. `Components/StarRating.razor` - interactive star rating widget


8. Update `NavMenu.razor` with the new routes and clean up the old example pages
9. Register the recipe service in `Program.cs` so it's available throughout the app

data.content:
Build succeeded with zero errors and warnings. Here's what was created:

**Project: `RecipeCommunity`** — a Blazor Web App with Server interactivity

| Page | Route | Render Mode |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage | `/` | Static SSR — category grid with icons |
| Category | `/category/{name}` | Static SSR — recipe list with star previews |
| Recipe Detail | `/recipe/{id}` | Static SSR + interactive `StarRating` widget |
| Submit Recipe | `/submit` | Interactive Server — form with validation |

**Key components:**
- **`StarRating.razor`** — interactive 1–5 star widget with hover effects and confirmation
- **`RecipeService`** — singleton in-memory store with 8 seed recipes across 6 categories
- **`RecipeFormModel`** — validated form model (title, category, ingredients, steps)

Most pages render as static HTML; only the rating widget and submit form opt into `@rendermode InteractiveServer`.
